business_segments.name: Memory
business_segments.description: DRAM, NAND Flash, HBM (High Bandwidth Memory)

business_segments.name: System LSI
business_segments.description: Exynos SoCs, image sensors, display ICs

business_segments.name: Foundry
business_segments.description: Contract chip manufacturing, advanced nodes (3nm GAA)

abf_ic_substrate_activity.abf_usage: Samsung is a major consumer of ABF substrates for its Exynos SoCs, HBM packaging, and foundry services. Uses ABF substrates from Samsung Electro-Mechanics, Ibiden, and Unimicron.
abf_ic_substrate_activity.cowos_details: Samsung's I-Cube and X-Cube are direct competitors to TSMC CoWoS. Samsung has been expanding capacity to capture AI chip packaging demand.

hbm_activity.market_position: Second largest HBM maker after SK Hynix
hbm_activity.notes: Samsung has been aggressively expanding HBM capacity to compete with SK Hynix. HBM3E qualification with NVIDIA completed in 2025.

foundry_activity.capacity_expansion: Taylor, Texas fab under construction; Pyeongtaek expansion

strengths: Leading HBM technology (HBM3E qualified with NVIDIA)
Massive capex capacity ($35B+ annual semiconductor capex)
weaknesses: Foundry yield issues at advanced nodes (3nm GAA)
Late to HBM3E vs SK Hynix
Lower foundry market share vs TSMC
opportunities: AI chip boom driving HBM and advanced packaging demand
US fab subsidies (CHIPS Act) for Taylor, TX fab
threats: TSMC dominance in foundry and advanced packaging
Chinese memory makers (CXMT, YMTC) gaining ground

abf_relevance.notes: Major ABF substrate consumer, HBM maker, CoWoS competitor via I-Cube/X-Cube

data_completeness.notes: Financial data approximate, missing detailed quarterly breakdowns

source_quality.notes: T3 - mostly training knowledge, 2 accessible URLs with limited data

financial_data.notes: Revenue and market cap approximate but reasonable

market_position.notes: Clear market position in HBM, foundry, packaging

review.decision_reason: Score 68 >= 60 threshold. Samsung is a critical ABF ecosystem player but T3 source quality requires human verification of financial figures and HBM market position.
